zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Croxton Carter於1490年出生在英國賀特福爾沃特福德時，他的父親Sir John William Nicholas de Carter15歲，母親Alice Ann Croxton28歲。他於1525年與Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier在英國賀特福爾沃特福德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年9月29日在他的家鄉享年68歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 迪奧尼斯 巴其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Croxton Carter于1490年出生在英国賀特福爾沃特福德时，他的父亲Sir John William Nicholas de Carter15岁，母亲Alice Ann Croxton28岁。他于1525年与Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier在英国賀特福爾沃特福德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1558年9月29日在他的家乡享年68岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可倫敦 聖 迪奧尼斯 巴其。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: shortened form of   McCarter  .
Americanized form of German   Karter   ‘carder’.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .
Americanized form of German Kartmann (see Kartman ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: Croxton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places in Cambridgeshire, Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Leicestershire, Norfolk, and Staffordshire named Croxton, from the Old Norse personal name Krókr (see Crook 3) or an Old English word crōc ‘nook’ + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

variants.name: Crowston
meanings: English: habitational name from South Croxton or Croxton Kerrial (Leicestershire), traditionally pronounced /'krousən/; or possibly from a village so named in Lincolnshire. Compare   Croxton  .

variants.name: Crook
meanings: English:

topographic name from Old Norse krókr ‘nook or bend’ denoting someone who lived by a bend in a river or road. In some instances the surname may have arisen as a habitational name from any of the places called Crook in Cumberland and Durham, or Crooke in Lancashire, all of which are derived from this word.

topographic name for someone who lived ‘(at the) barrow or mound’ from Brittonic crüg. In some instances this may have arisen as a habitational name from either Crook in Combe Raleigh or perhaps Crooke in North Tawton (both Devon), which are derived from this word.

variants.name: Croston
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from a place in Lancashire named Croston, from Old Norse kross ‘cross’ or Old English cros + Old English tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.
English: occasionally, a variant of   Croxton   or   Croson  .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Croxton Carter was born in 1490, in Watford, Hertfordshire, England, his father, Sir John William Nicholas de Carter, was 15 and his mother, Alice Ann Croxton, was 28. He married Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier in 1525, in Watford, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 29 September 1558, in his hometown, at the age of 68, and was buried in London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Croxton Carter est né en 1490, à Watford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John William Nicholas de Carter, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Alice Ann Croxton, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier en 1525, à Watford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 29 septembre 1558, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Croxton Carter è nato il nel 1490 nel Watford, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John William Nicholas de Carter, aveva 15 anni e il suo madre, Alice Ann Croxton, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier nel 1525, nel Watford, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 29 settembre 1558, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Croxton Carter(이)가 1490년 영국 허트퍼드실르 왓퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John William Nicholas de Carter(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Ann Croxton(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1525년 영국 허트퍼드실르 왓퍼드에서 Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1558년 9월 29일 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 런던 스트 디오니스 바ckc후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.St Mary's Church, Watford, Hertfordshire: St Mary's Church, Watford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex,: London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Croxton Carter nasceu em 1490, em Watford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John William Nicholas de Carter, tinha 15 anos e sua mãe, Alice Ann Croxton, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier em 1525, em Watford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de setembro de 1558, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos, e foi sepultado em London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary's Church, Watford, Hertfordshire: ст мэри'с чурч, ватфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex,: лондон ст дионис баккчурч, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Croxton Carter родился в 1490 г. в местности ватфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John William Nicholas de Carter было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Ann Croxton, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier в 1525 г. в местности ватфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 29 сентября 1558 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности лондон ст дионис баккчурч, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Croxton Carter nació en 1490, en Watford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John William Nicholas de Carter, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Alice Ann Croxton, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Lady Joan Elizabeth Bourchier en 1525, en Watford, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 29 de septiembre de 1558, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en London St Dionis Backchurch, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
